summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard captures a fiery 1985 performance highlighted by a scorching Cryptical > Other One > Cryptical sequence, soaring Eyes of the World and a blistering Dire Wolf.
review: The SBD source is remarkably clean – reviewers rave about its crystal‑clear mix with only occasional “shaky” moments that can be fixed by swapping channels. Musically the show shines in the second set, especially the intense Cryptical > Other One > Cryptical sandwich, a soaring Eyes of the World and powerful renditions of Dire Wolf and Comes A Time, though some feel Jerry’s vocals sag on certain numbers and the first‑set Eye jam runs too fast for others.

ai_rating.rationale: The performance is generally praised with several standout moments, while a minority of reviewers note vocal and pacing issues; the recording is consistently described as clear and high‑quality, leading to an overall strong but not flawless rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear mix praised by most reviewers; a few note occasional shakiness, the need to swap channels on TMNS>Don't Ease, and a less‑than‑perfect splice in Space→Nobody's Fault.

poor_songs: Eyes of the World (some say it runs too fast)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is described as a classic ’85 opener, while the second set flips into high energy with an intense Cryptical sandwich and soaring jams; pacing is praised overall but a few listeners felt the eye jam was rushed.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar work is called "hot" and "on fire" by several reviewers, but his vocal performance on Cryptical and some eye‑jam moments is criticized as weak or wasted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboard) gets quirky mentions – “Bobby lovelight rap”, “Brent don't need love anymore” – generally seen as part of the show’s character rather than a performance issue.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming is noted for driving the high‑energy sections; one reviewer mentions drums kicking in hard during Eyes, adding intensity.
